recent_trades.summary: Initiating ORCL position at 5% target on +2.7% gain, positive AI infrastructure sentiment, and upbeat Q4 preview, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL shows +0.12 sentiment with headlines on AI focus and market relevance; candlestick indicates strong green bar near 30D highs while technicals and news have not been fully priced into our portfolio. Fits current AI momentum theme, no prior trades on the name this period, and respects all position limits.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting entire MA position due to mild unrealized loss, lack of positive catalysts, and to free capital for higher-conviction AI plays, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA sits at 2.95% portfolio weight with -1.04% unrealized PL and no standout headlines amid the tech rally; recent exit of V on similar financial-sector caution supports rotation. This keeps us under the 20% single-name cap and 50-position limit while funding new exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Goldman Sachs to 8% on positive valuation commentary and strategic realignments within its digital asset portfolio, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Goldman Sachs is trading with strong relative intraday strength (+0.9%). Market commentary highlighting its attractive valuation and proactive restructuring of its crypto portfolio warrants an increase from a 5% to an 8% allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Broadcom to reach a 10% allocation as the stock exhibits relative strength and nears a 52-week high with positive sentiment, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom continues to show intraday relative strength (+0.8%) and is approaching its 52-week highs. Strong analyst commentary and inclusion in top stock picks justify bringing this position up to a full 10% core weight.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Amazon to an 8% weight as institutional buying and international expansion provide fundamental tailwinds, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Amazon is seeing positive momentum, further validated by reports of institutional accumulation (Cathie Wood) and news of its premium beauty expansion in India. Upsizing the position from 5% to 8% capitalizes on these solid fundamentals.

recent_trades.summary: Selling USO to consolidate energy exposure entirely into Exxon Mobil and free up cash for higher-conviction equity trades, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: With USO lagging and facing intraday weakness (-1.2%), holding direct crude exposure is redundant given our ~10% allocation to Exxon Mobil. Liquidating this position frees up approximately 5% of the portfolio to reallocate toward stronger momentum plays in tech and financials.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting WMT — hitting fresh 30-day lows at $117.39 with -1.2% decline today despite positive sentiment, freeing up capital for stronger positions.
recent_trades.reasoning: WMT is at its 30-day low and declining -1.2% today. The chart shows a clear downtrend over the past week. Despite positive Cramer comments, price action is weak. The position is small at 3.9% with a -1.21% unrealized loss. Better to redeploy this ~$4,465 into higher-conviction names.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming COP again as energy relative strength lags and its news flow remains neutral, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: COP continues to sit near the lower end of its 30-day range with a negative 30-day return, and its intraday chart still looks weaker than the industrial and semiconductor names competing for capital. Unlike XOM, it lacks a fresh supportive catalyst today and sentiment is slightly negative-to-neutral. This is only a partial reduction because the position is small already and I do not want to overtrade the same ticker within the session.

recent_trades.summary: Adding AMD on strong breakout momentum and supportive AI-market headlines, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is the strongest chart in the current holdings set, up 5.4% over 30 days and trading essentially at its 30-day high, which suggests continued momentum rather than exhaustion at this stage. News flow remains constructive with coverage highlighting a larger AI opportunity and participation in the AMD AI Developer Program. The add is modest because the name has already run sharply and the portfolio is nearly fully invested.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating NEM on +3.1% move today with positive sentiment (+0.27), strong 30-day performance (+3.09%), and gold sector strength as a safe haven amid US-Iran tensions.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEM is hitting 30-day highs (+3.1% today, +3.09% on the month) with positive sentiment driven by SeekingAlpha coverage. Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran escalation per Reuters) support gold and gold miners as safe havens. FCX is already up strongly; NEM provides complementary exposure to the metals/commodities trend. 5% target weight is appropriate for a new position.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting CMCSA as it's the only losing position (-0.37%) with minimal weight (0.85%) and weak price action (-0.6% today), freeing cash for higher-conviction opportunities.
recent_trades.reasoning: CMCSA was initiated yesterday on network expansion news but is down both today and from purchase price. At only 0.85% portfolio weight, it's too small to meaningfully contribute while tying up a position slot. Sentiment is only +0.27 and price action is negative. Better to recycle this into names with stronger momentum and catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Freeport-McMoRan as the stock benefits from copper price momentum and electrification tailwinds.
recent_trades.reasoning: FCX is up 2.1% today, supported by Wall Street upgrades citing the long-term electrification boom. This provides our portfolio with diversified materials exposure outside of our core energy and defense holdings.

recent_trades.summary: Correcting the Qualcomm position size to properly reflect an 8% target weight amid a broader AI and semiconductor rally.
recent_trades.reasoning: A prior execution left QCOM significantly under-weighted at 0.29%. We are upsizing this to the intended 8% to capture its strong intraday momentum (+3.1%) and benefit from the peer-led software and semiconductor surge.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Eli Lilly to a 12% weight following CVS coverage expansion for Zepbound and new positive clinical data.
recent_trades.reasoning: Fundamental drivers are overwhelmingly positive with CVS adding Zepbound to its formularies and new obesity data raising industry standards. Adding to our existing 7.17% position to maximize upside as the stock pushes higher intraday.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Comcast to reallocate capital, as the stock struggles to gain momentum and lags the broader communications sector.
recent_trades.reasoning: Despite localized broadband expansion news, Comcast's price action remains sluggish with a -0.55% 30-day decline. Capital is better deployed into areas with immediate high-growth catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AMD from 2.1% to 4% — strongest mover in universe today at +3.2%, hitting fresh 30-day highs with Snowflake AI frenzy lifting semis.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is surging +3.2% to fresh 30-day highs at $512.71. The Snowflake +35% AI frenzy headline is fueling a broad software/semi rally. AMD benefits directly from AI compute demand. Increasing from small 2.1% position to 4%.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to UNH from 5% to 6% on Bernstein price target raise and EPS recovery outlook, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Bernstein raising price target with EPS recovery outlook is a concrete positive catalyst. Stock is down slightly today (-0.2%) which gives a decent entry. Digital transformation analysis report also highlights growth initiatives. Adding modestly from 5% to 6%.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AVGO on continued AI leadership and price action holding near a fresh 30-day high, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO is one of the stronger semiconductor charts in the universe, up nearly 1% intraday and trading just under its 30-day high after a recent breakout. This aligns with the broader AI/software risk-on tape, and even after the add the position remains modestly sized, which fits prudent intraday scaling.

recent_trades.summary: Cutting COP modestly as energy price action weakens and its news flow has cooled to neutral, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: COP is down over the past month, trading near the lower end of its 30-day range, and today’s headlines are neutral rather than supportive. With USO also weak intraday and XOM benefiting from a clearer positive catalyst, I prefer to scale back COP rather than maintain a larger energy allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming TJX again because it remains one of the weakest charts in the portfolio despite only mildly positive news, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX is sitting near the bottom of its 30-day range with negative 30-day performance and soft intraday action versus stronger alternatives already held. News sentiment is slightly positive but not strong enough to offset the relative weakness, so I want to reduce this lagging small position and redeploy capital.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY to reach 7% on expanded CVS coverage for Zepbound and new obesity data that could set a higher industry bar.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY shows +1.7% intraday strength with positive 0.27 sentiment and multiple analyst notes calling it a safe long-term hold; technicals remain above the 30D low with room to run. This modestly boosts healthcare exposure while respecting the recent decision history and concentration limits.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing DIS to 6% target on very positive sentiment from the Disney-Philips MRI storytelling partnership and Carousel of Progress revamp.
recent_trades.reasoning: Headlines show fundamental innovation in healthcare storytelling and park attractions that should support consumer discretionary spending; the +0.5% candle holds above the 20-period SMA with low volatility. Position sizing keeps it below 20% cap while reallocating from cash without exceeding daily trade budget.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating AMD on +2% move today driven by Wall Street recognition of bigger market opportunity and AMD AI Developer Program expansion for defense applications.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is the strongest performer today (+2%) with dual catalysts: acceptance of Rocket One into AMD's AI Developer Program for defense/space applications and Wall Street recognizing a bigger market opportunity. Sentiment is positive (+0.14). 30D% is +2.03% showing sustained momentum. Starting a 7% position as AMD represents a high-quality semiconductor name not currently in the portfolio.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting MA as it's the only losing position (-0.34%) in the portfolio and sentiment (+0.10) doesn't provide strong enough conviction to justify holding over alternatives.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA is the sole position with negative unrealized P&L (-0.34%) and only mildly positive sentiment. The BitLicense approval catalyst from yesterday has been absorbed. The '2 Oversold Stocks Primed to Rebound' headline may refer to MA but without confirmation. Freeing up ~5% cash to redeploy into higher-conviction opportunities or hold as dry powder.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Nvidia as it lags peers and shows signs of short-term momentum exhaustion.
recent_trades.reasoning: Despite broader AI enthusiasm, Nvidia has underperformed its recent 30-day highs and is slightly down today. Shifting semiconductor exposure toward Qualcomm, which is displaying much stronger immediate momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Closing AbbVie to consolidate healthcare exposure entirely into Eli Lilly.
recent_trades.reasoning: With Eli Lilly demonstrating dominant catalysts in the healthcare sector, holding AbbVie introduces unnecessary opportunity cost. Exiting to fund the expansion of the LLY position.

recent_trades.summary: Fully exiting Meta Platforms to free up capital for higher-conviction opportunities.
recent_trades.reasoning: Meta's price action has flatlined recently, and its news flow is relatively muted compared to semiconductor and healthcare peers. Reallocating this capital allows for better risk-adjusted returns elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating an 8% position in Qualcomm to capture physical AI tailwinds and strong intraday momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: Qualcomm is showing robust relative strength, up 2.3% today on positive sentiment surrounding its physical AI applications. Initiating this position introduces a high-momentum growth driver to replace stalling semiconductor exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating small DIS position at 3% — stock at fresh 30-day highs with very positive sentiment (+0.53) and steady uptrend, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS is at 30-day highs ($104.35) with the highest sentiment score in our universe at +0.53. Steady 30-day uptrend of +0.58%. Positive news flow around theme parks and partnerships. Small position to capture the momentum without excessive risk.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming NVDA to 4% as it lags the semiconductor rally today with neutral sentiment and no fresh positive catalysts.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is -0.2% while AMD (+2%) and QCOM (+2.3%) advance on AI momentum; neutral 0.00 sentiment, -2.54% unrealized loss, and headlines focused on competitors rather than NVDA itself justify partial exit. Reduces from 7% to free capital and lower portfolio volatility.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% DIS position on very positive sentiment from Disney-Phillips MRI storytelling partnership and Carousel of Progress revamp.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS is up 0.6% today with a strong green candle and closing near session highs; 0.53 sentiment score is the highest in the universe today and headlines indicate fundamental innovation not yet reflected in the modest 30D return. New position sized conservatively to 5% given no current holding and to respect 20% single-name cap.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to QCOM on fresh AI-related positive coverage and a breakout push to the top of its 30-day range, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM is up 2.34% over 30 days and trading just under its 30-day high, with today's candlestick showing strong upside follow-through. News sentiment is positive and tied to AI/physical AI exposure, which provides a fresher catalyst than many slower mega-cap peers. The position is currently very small at 0.53%, so increasing only to 3% improves sizing without overcommitting.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Exxon Mobil (XOM) to 3% target weight on positive oil price outlook and higher price target from Mizuho, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: XOM currently at 2.54% weight; adding from proceeds of NEM sale to reach 3%. Benefiting from geopolitical tensions and analyst upgrades, with limited downside due to strong balance sheet and integrated model.

recent_trades.summary: Selling Newmont (NEM) to reallocate into energy, as gold lacks near-term catalysts and US-Iran tensions favor oil, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEM is down 2.72% with no immediate news drivers, while oil prices are expected to rise due to escalating US-Iran conflict, benefiting XOM. Selling NEM frees up capital for a high-conviction energy position and aligns the portfolio with geopolitical realities.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to RTX to reach an 8% weight, supported by positive news on Javelin deliveries and the broader need for defense systems.
recent_trades.reasoning: As geopolitical stability deteriorates, RTX's defense portfolio becomes increasingly critical. Recent deliveries from its Javelin joint venture confirm strong operational momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Lockheed Martin to a 10% weight as defense primes are natural beneficiaries of escalating global conflicts.
recent_trades.reasoning: Supported by highly positive sentiment (+0.31) and structural tailwinds from global rearmament needs during the US-Iran confrontation, LMT offers safety and upside.

recent_trades.summary: Doubling Exxon Mobil to a 10% weight following Mizuho's upgraded target and massive macro tailwinds for energy producers.
recent_trades.reasoning: Exxon is a premier defensive asset in this environment. The combination of strong analyst upgrades and severe Middle East supply threats makes it a high-conviction holding.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in the US Oil Fund to directly capture the impending spike in crude prices driven by US-Iran air strikes.
recent_trades.reasoning: With headlines indicating that oil is 'about to go vertical' due to the unravelling ceasefire and escalating conflict, USO provides a direct hedge against geopolitical supply disruptions.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Amazon from ~10% to 5% to reallocate capital toward defensive energy and aerospace assets.
recent_trades.reasoning: While Amazon has robust fundamentals, its e-commerce segment is exposed to higher shipping costs and depressed consumer sentiment in a geopolitical crisis. Trimming limits downside while preserving cloud exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Closing the Mastercard position to free up capital and reduce exposure to potential consumer spending headwinds.
recent_trades.reasoning: Inflationary pressures from expected oil price spikes will likely dampen consumer discretionary spending, reducing transaction volumes for payment networks.

recent_trades.summary: Fully exiting Caterpillar to remove cyclical industrial exposure as global growth expectations are threatened by energy shocks.
recent_trades.reasoning: Caterpillar's reliance on global economic expansion makes it highly susceptible to the negative macro implications of a sustained US-Iran conflict and rising oil prices.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Tesla completely to de-risk the portfolio from high-beta consumer discretionary exposure amid rising geopolitical tensions.
recent_trades.reasoning: Tesla is vulnerable to supply chain disruptions and broader market sell-offs stemming from the escalating Middle East conflict. The risk-reward is no longer favorable, and capital is better utilized in defensive sectors.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting INTC at a -4.4% loss — stock hitting fresh 30-day lows at $117.28 with negative sentiment and no catalyst for reversal, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC has been in a clear downtrend from $123 to $117 over the past month, now sitting at the 30-day low. Sentiment is slightly negative (-0.09). The position was initiated just this morning as a speculative play on pre-market momentum, but the stock has continued declining. Cut the loss early rather than let it compound.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting entire V position on negative -0.07 sentiment and limited catalysts relative to peers in current geopolitical environment.
recent_trades.reasoning: V's recent news is non-material (soccer partnership) while sentiment lags the broader payments sector; small -0.22% unrealized loss and 3.99% weight make full exit prudent to redeploy capital. Technicals show weak red candle and no breakout relative to MA, aligning with caution on market-wide negative sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMZN to 8% target on strong +0.37 sentiment, Cathie Wood bargain buying, and India premium beauty expansion news.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN's positive news flow and analyst attention appear underpriced given only -0.15% daily move and +1.57% unrealized gain; raising from 5.45% weight adds conviction without concentration risk. Technicals show support near 267-268 with positive longer-term trend intact despite market-wide Iran headlines.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the small remaining UNP position because it continues to lag the stronger industrial names already in the portfolio, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNP is still well below its 30-day high and remains one of the softer industrial charts in the current book, even though headline tone is mildly positive. Since the position is only 1.00% after a recent trim, the opportunity cost of keeping it is higher than the diversification benefit. I prefer to recycle that capital into higher-conviction names rather than repeatedly nurse a marginal holding.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY on favorable obesity-drug coverage news and price action pressing near 30-day highs, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY has one of the strongest news stacks in the set today, with CVS coverage expansion for Zepbound and supportive commentary around new obesity data. Technically it is trading just under its 30-day high and showing relative strength despite a cautious market backdrop. The position is currently only 2.76% of the portfolio, so raising it to 5% is a measured add rather than an overextension.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Goldman Sachs to 3% after crypto portfolio shakeup signal confidence in digital assets.
recent_trades.reasoning: GS is currently 1.99% of the portfolio. The news of a significant crypto portfolio reallocation shows strategic initiative and could attract investor interest. With positive sentiment and an underweight position, adding to 3% is a measured step.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Oracle to 3% on positive market commentary and stable price action.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL is underweight at 2.04% and the news today highlights it as a key market mover. While the catalyst is less specific than AVGO or LMT, the positive sentiment and our underweight status warrant a top-up to our standard 3% allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Buying Broadcom to 3% weight as AI trade enters second wave and technicals flash a perfect rating.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO is underweight at 1.52% and has just scored a rare perfect technical rating with a breakout setup nearing completion. The AI semiconductor theme continues to drive upside. Increasing to 3% captures momentum with strong technical confirmation.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

mvp_trade.summary: Fully exiting Meta Platforms to free up capital for higher-conviction opportunities.

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.50%, Nasdaq +0.67%, Dow -0.02%. Technology led sectors (+2.09%) while Consumer Staples lagged (-1.00%). DeepSeek V4 Pro led the day at -0.11%. 0 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek V4 Pro at +22.41%. Key headline: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan Chase could spend $20 billion on acquisition: 'We are on the lookout’ - CNBC — market reaction muted.
market_brief.key_moves: ORCL +6.4% (held by 2/6) | AMD +6.1% (held by 4/6) | QCOM +5.5% (held by 3/6)
